characters.description: The leader of Fortune's Folly is an exceptionally durable dimensional mage and protector. His abilities include Soul Sight, Reveal, teleportation, defensive auras, and access to a pocket Delve shared with Grotto. He is bonded to both Grotto and the wider dangers surrounding the system.
characters.name: Arlo Xor'Drel

characters.description: A Delve Core bonded to Arlo and resident in their shared pocket Delve. Grotto can speak into Arlo's mind, manifest a physical form, and alter the Delve under his control.
characters.name: Grotto

characters.description: A cleric and frontline fighter in Arlo's party. Xim combines healing, shields, and divine fire with an ascended bestial combat form.
characters.name: Xim

characters.description: A Ravvenblaq swordsman who serves as one of the party's principal frontline fighters. Varrin is a politically connected Delver whose family carries knowledge and obligations concerning Hiward.
characters.name: Varrin

characters.description: An Eschendur native who serves the party as an alchemist, archer, and scout. Nuralie seeks enough strength to return home and protect it.
characters.name: Nuralie

characters.description: A divine-made clay construct who became a Delver and developed a humanlike form. Etja uses force magic, mana shaping, Nullify, and Shared Vessel. Her inherited memories and the abilities built into her origin remain closely connected to Orexis.
characters.name: Etja

characters.description: Arlo's resilient extradimensional summon and a recurring combat ally. Shog Tuatha recovers and changes while in its home dimension.
characters.name: Shog Tuatha

characters.description: A dangerous but apparently helpful power linked to Arlo's unusual eye-derived abilities and his Traveler's Amulet. Fortune's gifts have aided Arlo, but the nature and intentions behind that aid remain uncertain.
characters.name: Fortune

characters.description: A hostile divine influence connected to Etja's creation and to the party's continuing fears. A fragment of Orexis's soul was once hidden within Etja, leaving the entity central to her history and to the group's concern about powers operating beyond ordinary Delvers.
characters.name: Orexis

characters.description: Xim's father and an elder of her Third Layer community.
characters.name: Drel'Gethed

characters.description: A level-10, system-aware Architect whose body encompasses the lesser mimics throughout its Delve. Ancient, intelligent, and recently awakened, it recognizes unusual qualities in Arlo's party but initially refuses to explain the card or the system's phased rollout.
characters.name: Mimic Architect

characters.description: A gold-level Littan Delver sent by a duchess to investigate a Littan party member's death. He questions Arlo about Varrin's involvement and appears able to judge whether the answers are truthful.
characters.name: Littan investigator

characters.description: The Littan investigator's gold-level associate. He uses shadow magic to distract or conceal Arlo's companions and can dispel Xim's flames.
characters.name: Littan shadow associate

characters.description: Xim's mother and a member of her Third Layer tribal community.
characters.name: Xim's mother

characters.description: Xim's divine mother figure and the goddess of her Third Layer tribe. She receives Arlo in her sanctuary and counsels him about his unusual gifts, avatars, and advancement through the system.
characters.name: Xim's goddess

characters.description: A level-23 Delver of Xim's tribe who practices dream forging. She agrees to assess Arlo's need for a replacement weapon.
characters.name: Tribal dream forger

characters.description: Varrin's grandfather and the patriarch of House Ravvenblaq. A level-56 Delver, he brings Fortune's Folly into his family's response to the growing avatar crisis and warns them that their connection to an earlier avatar incident will affect Hiward's public policy.
characters.name: Ealdric Ravvenblaq Sr.

characters.description: Varrin's older brother and a Delver in another Ravvenblaq party. Ealdric Ravvenblaq IV is commonly called Drift by his companions. His usual combat work emphasizes control magic, while his family position makes him part of the political response to the avatar threat.
characters.name: Ealdric Ravvenblaq IV

characters.description: The official leading Central briefs the party on the three known special Delves and identifies the two lower-level sites as possible routes toward Phase 2. Central will not formally support the effort because wider Delver creation could conflict with Hiward's interests, but the director promises limited diplomatic help with passage through the Littan blockade.
characters.name: Director of Central

characters.description: The Littan naval commander responsible for enforcing the blockade of Eschendur. He rejects the party's newly issued passage documents, demands the surrender of Nuralie, and orders Fortune's Folly detained even though the stated grounds conflict with their valid authorization.
characters.name: Littan admiral

characters.description: The admiral commanding the Littan blockade force. Richton underestimates the threat posed by Fortune's Folly and continues to dispute the party's valid authorization. He orders a temporary fallback and rescue operation after losing four ships.
characters.name: Admiral Richton

characters.description: A level-17 imperial naval Delver commanding concealed experimental caravels. He attacks both Fortune's Folly and Littan personnel and tries to frame Arlo's group. His specialized movement effects and advanced weapons make him the central threat in the sea battle.
characters.name: Littan gold captain

characters.description: An Eschendur ruler and a religious authority associated with the water deity. The Zenithar recognizes the party's lawful passage, grants them asylum, and rejects the Littan blockade commander's attempt to seize them within Eschendur's waters.
characters.name: Zenithar of the water deity

characters.description: A level-15, platinum-ranked Dajinin Zenithar who arrives through Arlo's portal after the evacuation of Nuralie's village. Her soul appears stronger than that of the Zenithar the party previously met.
characters.name: Dajinin Zenithar

characters.description: A level-24 Architect that overwhelms nearby Littan Delvers, notices Fortune's Folly, and seizes Arlo by the throat.
characters.name: the Operator
